content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      : File (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18419)                Date  : July 20, 1978




From  : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
          Barbara Campbell

Subject: Advice given to Laura Worsinger, J.C. Penney Co.

          Laura Worsinger (213-620-1740) with the Western
Regional Counsel's office of J.C. Penney Co. called
today to ask about the use of "Penney Pac" for a Pac
sponsored by the J.C. Penney Co.

          2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18419(b)(1) requires that
the Pac's name include the official or publicly recog-
nized name of the sponsoring organization.

          After discussing the matter with Ted, Lee and
Mike, I advised her that the use of "Penney Pac" by
J.C. Penney is within the provisions of 2 Cal. Adm.
Code Section 18419(b)(1).



BC:plh

State of California                                                    Franchise Tax Board

Memorandum

To    : Michael Bennett, Executive Director          Date  : September 22, 1978
       Fair Political Practices Commission
       1100 K Street
       Sacramento, CA 95814
                                                     File No.:

                                                     Telephone: ATSS (    )
                                                                (    )


From  : Robert C. Summers

Subject: AB-3667                                     A-78-3020

       This memo is to highlight areas that we have identified in the random audit
       selection process provided by this bill.

LOBBYIST AUDITS

1. A determination must be made identifying all lobbyists registered
   during the calendar year 1978.

2. Those not in substantial compliance and those which are new
   lobbyists, not previously audited, must be identified.

3. Twenty-five percent (25%) of the remainder (Item 1 minus
   Item 2) are to be selected.

4. The individuals to be audited are those selected at Item 3 and
   those identified at Item 2. The period for the first audit will
   be the calendar year 1978.

CAMPAIGNS

1. Identification of all statewide candidates who spent less than
   $25,000. A sample of 10% of these is required to be audited
   under Section 90001(b). The Franchise Tax Board, of course,
   would not audit the candidates of the State Board of Equalization
   selected in this process. I assume that these would be done by
   the Fair Political Practices Commission.

   We are not certain whether the 10% sample is to be taken of each
   group of candidates by office mentioned or of the collective groups.

2. A random selection must be made of 50% of the contested Senate
   districts, 50% of the Assembly districts and 50% of the contested
   offices for Superior Court Judge. The determination of amounts
   spent is not necessary for this selection process.

Michael Bennett
September 22, 1978
Page 2


3.  Identification of all committees who have spent more than
     $10,000 during the calendar year 1978, other than controlled
     or measure committees, so that a random selection of 50% can
     be made as required by Section 90001(g).

4.  We would like a definition of the term "primarily supporting"
     for the purpose of identifying audits required by Section
     90001(e).

TIMING

1.  For the lobbyist audits, I assume that the selection process
     will not be made until after the last date for filing the last
     statement for the calendar year 1978, which would be January 31,
     1979.  All of the steps, except the actual selection, can be
     done prior to that time.  We would like the selection to take
     place as soon as possible after January 31, 1979.

2.  For the campaign audits, the law provides that the selection
     process cannot be made until after the final filing date of the
     post-election statement, or January 31, 1979.  A substantial part
     of the process necessary to make the selection can be accomplished
     prior to that date, however, the final identification of those
     committees, whose selection is based on a monetary amount, cannot
     be made until after the final statement is analyzed.  We are, of
     course, interested in having this workload available as soon as
     possible.

     It appears to me that the major problem is the identification of 90001(g)
     committees whose audit depends upon an expenditure requirement.  This process
     requires that the entire universe be identified and the amount spent be
     determined prior to the selection.  Delays in the selection process will
     result in a further delay in the performance of the audit.

     We would be happy to discuss these problems with you or a member of your staff
     at your earliest convenience.


[signature]

Chief, Political Reform Audit Division

cc: Martin Huff
     Doug Dick
     Norman Fuller
     Jim Turrini
     Roy Eto

sections.question_synthetic: This document consists of two separate memoranda: (1) FPPC advice to J.C. Penney regarding PAC naming conventions, and (2) Franchise Tax Board inquiry to FPPC regarding audit selection procedures for AB-3667.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that J.C. Penney's use of 'Penney Pac' complies with naming requirements under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18419(b)(1). The Franchise Tax Board outlined specific audit selection procedures and timing issues requiring FPPC coordination.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two separate memoranda with different dates (July 20 and September 22, 1978). The first is an internal FPPC memo documenting informal advice given. The second is incoming correspondence from the Franchise Tax Board. No formal question/conclusion sections present in either memo. Confidence reduced due to lack of standard advice letter formatting in both documents.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: This document consists of two separate memoranda: (1) FPPC advice to J.C. Penney regarding PAC naming conventions, and (2) Franchise Tax Board inquiry to FPPC regarding audit selection procedures for AB-3667.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised that J.C. Penney's use of 'Penney Pac' complies with naming requirements under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18419(b)(1). The Franchise Tax Board outlined specific audit selection procedures and timing issues requiring FPPC coordination.

embedding.summary: This document contains two separate memoranda from July and September 1978: an FPPC advice memorandum confirming that 'Penney Pac' complies with PAC naming regulations, and a Franchise Tax Board memorandum detailing audit selection procedures for lobbyists and campaigns under AB-3667, requesting FPPC clarification and coordination.
